commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `finalizeTransportStream` to throw `output.errorMessage` for errored or aborted streams, adds unit coverage, and also normalizes cron `agentId`/`sessionKey` string `"null"` values to clear signals.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main hardcodes the transport finalization error despite `failTransportStream` carrying `output.errorMessage`, and the linked cron path currently preserves non-empty string `"null"` values.
commands.review_followup: Not a safe automated repair candidate: the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer scope/compatibility review for the included cron fix.

target.title: fix(agents): thread errorMessage through finalizeTransportStream
target.body:
## Summary

`finalizeTransportStream` throws when a stream stops with `stopReason === "error"` or `"aborted"`. The thrown error was hardcoded to `"An unknown error occurred"`, silently discarding `output.errorMessage` — which `failTransportStream` always populates before marking the stream as failed. Every provider transport failure surfaced the same opaque string regardless of the actual cause.

The fix reads `output.errorMessage` at throw-time with a null-coalesce fallback, preserving the `"An unknown error occurred"` string for any path that bypasses `failTransportStream`.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: `finalizeTransportStream` throws `"An unknown error occurred"` regardless of the actual error, discarding `output.errorMessage` set at `transport-stream-shared.ts:133`.

Real environment tested: unit tests in `src/agents/transport-stream-shared.test.ts`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/transport-stream-shared.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: three new test cases cover the error path, abort path, and absent-errorMessage fallback — 11/11 pass.

Observed result after fix: `finalizeTransportStream` throws `output.errorMessage` when set; falls back to `"An unknown error occurred"` when `output.errorMessage` is absent.

What was not tested: live provider transport failures requiring a running provider environment.
target.author: martingarramon
